  • Changelog

Articles

Applied Track

Workflow-oriented walkthroughs from a five-minute landing example through anatomy of a fit, the metafor::escalc() bridge, grid choice, diagnostics, plotting, and a worked case study.

A1 · Getting started

A five-minute first fit on a five-site toy dataset.

A2 · Anatomy of a bayesEfron fit

Every field of the bef_fit_re object and every S3 method.

A3 · From metafor::escalc() to bayesEfron

End-to-end path from group-arm summaries through escalc to a fitted bayesEfron model.

A4 · Choosing a grid

Side-by-side comparison of the four grid recipes.

A5 · Diagnostics in practice

How to read diagnose() and decide whether a fit is releasable.

A6 · Plotting and visualization

Four diagnostic-and-summary views of a fitted bayesEfron model.

A7 · Case study — Lee–Sui replication light

One K=50 replication of the Lee–Sui benchmark, end to end.

Methodological Track

Mathematical background — the empirical-Bayes deconvolution problem, the Efron log-spline prior, the random-effects hierarchy, grid construction and spline basis, the Stan model and cache, and the verification ladder that gates the v0.1 release.

M1 · The empirical-Bayes deconvolution problem

The Robbins-Efron formulation, prior-art landscape, and motivation for the fully Bayesian log-spline approach.

M2 · The Efron log-spline prior

Derivation, simplex normalisation, half-Cauchy hyperprior, and a smoothness sweep.

M3 · Bayesian random-effects hierarchy

The four-level hierarchy, posterior factorisation, and the package’s metadata payload.

M4 · Grid construction and spline basis

Mathematical specification of the four grid recipes and the natural-cubic-spline basis.

M5 · Stan model, sampler, and cache

The locked Stan model, the seven-field data block, and the two-tier compile cache.

M6 · Verification and calibration

The verification tier ladder and the Lee-Sui aggregate coverage evidence that gates v0.1.0.

On this page

Developed by JoonHo Lee.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
